message:
feat(fr-protocol): opt-in RESP3 downgrade parsing (br-frankenredis-ozcx)

Adds ParserConfig::allow_resp3 (default false). With the flag set,
each RESP3 type prefix downgrades into a RESP2-shaped RespFrame so
downstream RESP2-only code can consume trusted replies:

    %  map            → flat Array of 2N field/value entries
    ~  set            → Array
    >  push           → Array (discriminator stripped)
    #  bool           → Integer 0/1
    ,  double         → BulkString of the ASCII representation
    _  null           → BulkString(None)
    (  big number     → BulkString of the ASCII digits
    =  verbatim       → BulkString with the 4-byte "txt:" / "mkd:"
                        prefix stripped
    |  attribute      → attribute peeled off, next real frame returned
    !  blob error     → Error(utf8(payload))

With allow_resp3 left at the default `false`, every RESP3 prefix
still returns UnsupportedResp3Type — preserving the fail-closed
posture codified by fr_p2c_002_u007_resp3_fail_closed_prefix_matrix.

The live-oracle harness enables the flag via live_oracle_parser_config()
when reading replies from the vendored redis-server (HELLO 3 can
flip that connection to RESP3 for the rest of the run).

All existing ParserConfig struct-literal callsites (fr-persist AOF,
fr-runtime per-client, fr-conformance self-encoded parse) switched
to `..ParserConfig::default()` so they automatically inherit the
safe RESP2-only default.

Added fr-protocol unit test covering every RESP3 downgrade path.
fr-conformance 172/172 lib tests pass; fr-protocol 38+ tests;
fr-command 639; fr-persist 120; fr-runtime unaffected. The
core_connection live oracle test remains skipped pending the
harness read-timeout work tracked under br-frankenredis-4e32.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -3540,6 +3540,11 @@ impl Runtime {
                 .min(self.server.proto_max_bulk_len),
             max_array_len: self.policy.gate.max_array_len,
             max_recursion_depth: 128,
+            // Server-side parsing of CLIENT → SERVER command frames
+            // stays RESP2-only; the RESP3 downgrade helpers are
+            // reserved for trusted client-side replies (live-oracle
+            // harness). (br-frankenredis-ozcx)
+            ..fr_protocol::ParserConfig::default()
         }
     }
